case_id: 50 ham emails from easy_ham_2 subset, August 2002

pi_items.source: Task description instructs agent to create folder named 'Active-Threads'. Verifier checks for 'thread' or 'active' substring in folder name (case-insensitive). Consistent across task.json description, export_result.sh, and verifier.py.
Task description instructs agent to create folder named 'Security-Review'. Verifier checks for 'security' or 'review' substring. Consistent across all task files.
Setup script creates /home/ga/Documents directory and deletes stale report file. Export script reads this exact path. Verifier checks existence and content at this path. All consistent.
Task description says 'Draft an email to engineering-manager@company.com'. Verifier checks for this address in outgoing emails (Sent/Drafts folders). Consistent across description and verifier.
Counted 50 files matching ham_*.eml in examples/bluemail_env/assets/emails/ham/. Setup script loads ALL of them via glob. Independent count confirmed 50.

original_subjects: Re: [zzzzteana] Nothing like mama used to make
Re: [zzzzteana] Nothing like mama used to make
Re: [zzzzteana] Nothing like mama used to make

emails.subject: [IRR] Klez: The Virus That  Won't Die
Re: [SAdev] Live Rule Updates after Release ???
Re: Encryption approach to secure web applications

data_quirks.subject_normalization: Subjects require iterative stripping of Re:/RE:/Fwd: and [list-tag] prefixes. Some emails have nested patterns like '[ILUG] Re: Sun Solaris' where the list tag comes before Re:, requiring alternating passes. Trailing punctuation (periods, question marks) is also stripped.
data_quirks.sun_solaris_variants: The 'Sun Solaris' thread has 4 subject variants after normalization: 'RE: [ILUG] Sun Solaris..', '[ILUG] Sun Solaris..', '[ILUG] Re: Sun Solaris', and 'Re: [ILUG] Re: Sun Solaris'. All normalize to 'sun solaris' after iterative prefix/suffix stripping.
data_quirks.security_keyword_matching: Security classification uses subject-line-only keyword matching to avoid false positives. Keywords include SpamAssassin list identifiers 'satalk' and 'sadev' which appear in [SAtalk] and [SAdev] subject prefixes.
data_quirks.date_header_modification: Setup script replaces original 2002 Date headers with recent timestamps to ensure BlueMail displays the emails (its default 2-week sync window would otherwise exclude 2002 emails). This does not affect task correctness since the task does not reference email dates.
data_quirks.email_display_in_bluemail: BlueMail shows 8 emails per page in the inbox list. Subjects are truncated at ~35 characters with '...'. Full sender email addresses are only visible in the reading pane (right side), not the list view. The agent must click individual emails to read full From fields.
data_quirks.two_email_threads_not_included: Several 2-email threads exist (e.g., 'The case for spam' with 2 messages, 'eircoms adsl modems' with 2) but are below the 3-message threshold. The 'The case for spam' pair (ham_015, ham_035) is classified as security instead.

privileged_info_summary: The inbox contains 50 emails from the SpamAssassin public corpus. There are exactly 4 discussion threads with 3 or more messages: (1) 'Sun Solaris' with 6 messages from Fergal Moran, Kiall Mac Innes, John P. Looney, Padraig Brady, Mark Twomey, and Matthew French; (2) 'Nothing like mama used to make' with 3 messages from Stewart Smith and Martin Adamson; (3) 'Which Muppet Are You?' with 3 messages from uncle_slacky, Tim Chapman, and leslie ellen jones; (4) 'Entrepreneurs' with 3 messages from Manoj Kasichainula, John Evdemon, and Chuck Murcko. Total thread emails: 15. There are 9 security-related emails covering topics including Klez virus, SpamAssassin configuration and rule updates, spam filtering approaches, encryption for web applications, Habeas anti-spam verification, and mailing list virus issues. After moving thread emails to 'Active-Threads' and security emails to 'Security-Review', exactly 26 emails should remain in the inbox. The verification equation is 15 + 9 + 26 = 50. The most active thread is 'Sun Solaris' with 6 messages. The agent must create a report at ~/Documents/triage_report.txt listing each thread with participant full names and email addresses, and send a summary email to engineering-manager@company.com.
